name: src/runtime layout (V1)
description: src/runtime/ holds the AgentRuntime seam: runtime.ts (interface + SpawnContext/ResumeContext/ParseContext/PrepareContext), registry.ts (AgentRegistry, BUILT_IN_RUNTIMES), claude-code.ts (spawn-per-turn, stream-json), sapling.ts (spawn-per-turn, NDJSON), codex.ts (one-shot, raw-text), declarative.ts (AgentConfig → AgentRuntime), version.ts (shared install-check probe), parsers/{jsonl-claude,stream-json,raw-text}.ts.

title: Use Zod 4 for AgentConfig validation (and future burrow.toml schemas)
rationale: SPEC §6 lists Zod as the validation choice for burrow.toml, AgentConfig, sandbox config, and message envelopes. Phase 4 needed AgentConfig to land — adding zod here gets the dep in place once and unblocks Phase 8's burrow.toml + secrets work without a second decision. Zod 4 was chosen because the runtime was new code (no v3 callers to migrate).

content: Declarative AgentConfig args support these tokens: {{prompt}}, {{workspace}}, {{run_id}}, {{burrow_id}}. {{prompt}} expands to the steering-prefixed prompt (pending messages then the user prompt) — same shape that promptDelivery=stdin writes to stdin. Unknown tokens are left literal so a malformed template surfaces as the agent's own error rather than a silent substitution.

Steering messages render to '[STEERING] (priority:

) ' when prefixed onto a prompt (sapling/codex/declarative) or wrapped as a Claude Code user-text envelope (claude-code). This single text shape is what burrow surfaces to every runtime, regardless of how the inbox is delivered (stdin-ndjson vs prompt prefix). Phase 5's inbox layer should reuse encodeInboxMessage rather than re-deriving the format.

name: registry-last-write-wins
description: AgentRegistry.register(input) accepts either a raw AgentRuntime or an AgentConfig object. AgentConfigs go through loadAgentConfig (Zod validation → ValidationError on shape failure) so malformed configs throw at registration time, not at first run. Same id later overrides earlier (SPEC §12.3 resolution order: built-ins → ~/.config/burrow/agents.toml → project burrow.toml). New ids extend.

content: AgentRuntime.prepareWorkspace receives the host workspace path, not the in-sandbox /workspace path. The bwrap/sandbox-exec wrapper bind-mounts ctx.workspacePath to /workspace at exec time, so any setup files (e.g. .claude/settings.local.json, .burrow/codex/<run>.txt) must be written to the host path before spawn.

name: agent-config-parse-result-shape
description: parseAgentConfig returns AgentConfigParseResult { ok: boolean; config?: AgentConfig; errors?: AgentConfigParseError[] } — NOT the zod safeParse {success, data, error} shape. agents validate command checks result.ok and reads result.config / result.errors accordingly.

content: AgentRuntime.installCheck() must populate InstallCheckResult.path with the resolved host binary path (Bun.which for bare names, the absolute path otherwise). burrow up reads this to populate SandboxProfile.toolchainPaths so the sandbox can locate and read the binary; without it, sandbox-exec/bwrap fails the bare-name lookup. Built-ins inherit via runVersionCheck; declarative agents resolve config.command the same way.

title: claude-code uses two distinct credential channels: ~/.claude (local, plan billing) vs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (deploy, API billing)
rationale: claude-code's own auth precedence prefers ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env over the ~/.claude OAuth cache when both are present. Burrow leans on this: local sandboxes mount ~/.claude (burrow-980e) so dev runs bill against the user's Claude Pro/Max plan, while deployed targets (fly/docker/tarball, Phase 9) ship ANTHROPIC_API_KEY via [secrets] so unattended runs bill against the API tier. Don't forward the OAuth cache to remote/headless envs — the token is single-user-bound, plan rate limits aren't shaped for production agent loops, and refresh requires interactive /login which no remote machine has. No burrow.toml flag needed: the deploy environment dictates which channel is available, claude-code picks correctly. Document in SPEC §17.4 when burrow-980e lands.

name: agent-credential-forwarding
description: AgentRuntime.credentialPaths(): Promise<string[]> declares host paths to forward read-only into the sandbox (SPEC §17.4). burrow up calls credentialPaths() for every burrow.toml [[agents]] row whose runtime is registered, dedups in declaration order, and lands the result on SandboxProfile.readOnlyMounts. Per-agent opt-out via burrow.toml [[agents]] forwardCredentials = false (camelCase, schema-level z.boolean().optional() — undefined is treated as 'forward'). claude-code runtime returns existing [~/.claude, ~/.claude.json] paths, AND its prepareWorkspace copies host ~/.claude/.credentials.json to <workspace>/.claude/.credentials.json so the sandbox-resolved HOME=/workspace lookup finds it without a second /login. sapling/codex don't implement credentialPaths yet — they ship analogous caches later.

Initial host-credential forwarding for claude-code only handled the linux file path (~/.claude/.credentials.json), but on macOS Claude Code stores OAuth tokens in the macOS Keychain (security service: 'Claude Code-credentials') and the file doesn't exist on disk. The sandbox profile denies Keychain IPC, so claude inside the burrow reports 'Not logged in · Please run /login' even when the host is fully authenticated.
resolution: On darwin, prepareWorkspace shells out to 'security find-generic-password -s "Claude Code-credentials" -w' to read the OAuth blob from the Keychain, then writes it as <workspace>/.claude/.credentials.json (mode 0600). Claude Code's file fallback picks up the materialized creds. Pass plat + keychainReader as injected options on forwardClaudeHostCredentials() so tests can stub the Keychain without shelling out.

title: claude-code runtime spawns with --dangerously-skip-permissions
rationale: Burrow's bwrap/sandbox-exec sandbox is the actual enforcement boundary for any agent it hosts — Claude Code's interactive permission prompts are defense-in-depth designed for ungoverned hosts. Inside a non-interactive spawn the prompt has no human to respond and the agent deadlocks (observed: agent emitted 'The ml prime command needs your approval to run' text events and made no progress). The flag is added to both buildSpawnCommand and buildResumeCommand in src/runtime/claude-code.ts. Future agents/runtimes that exhibit similar interactive-confirmation behavior under burrow should bypass at spawn time for the same reason.

title: Per-burrow TMPDIR isolation for agents with UID-keyed shared tmp roots
rationale: claude-code's Bash tool stores command output under ${TMPDIR-/tmp}/claude-${uid}/<sanitized-cwd>/<session-id>/tasks/<task-id>.output and runs a startup-cleanup sweep across the entire UID-keyed root on every spawn. That root is shared with the host's claude-code session and every sibling burrow, so cleanup races produced intermittent <bash output unavailable: ... could not be read (EPERM)> failures (burrow-8452). The fix lives in the runtime: prepareWorkspace plants <workspace>/.burrow-tmp/ (with a '*' .gitignore so worktrees stay clean) and buildSpawnCommand/buildResumeCommand emit env: { TMPDIR: <in-sandbox workspace>/.burrow-tmp }. The in-sandbox workspace differs by sandbox primitive (bwrap remaps to /workspace; sandbox-exec keeps the host path), so claudeCodeBurrowTmpdir() branches on process.platform. No sandbox-profile change is needed — the workspace subpath is already file-write*. Apply the same pattern to any future runtime whose host tooling reaches into a shared ${TMPDIR-/tmp}/<tool>-${uid}/ root.

name: dogfood-baseline-2026-05-08
description:
End-to-end dogfooding session 2026-05-08 against burrow v0.2.0 on darwin/sandbox-exec — first known full exercise with a real claude-code agent.

## Setup
- Host: darwin 25.4.0, sandbox-exec primitive (no bwrap available).
- Project burrow: bur_3305dwv1s22a (active, branch burrow/bur_3305dwv1s22a).
- Forked task burrow: bur_2ydvj37tgjg7 on task/bur_2ydvj37tgjg7.
- Profile: network=restricted, allowed_domains=[npmjs, github.com, api.anthropic.com], timeoutMs=3600000, RO mounts of ~/.claude and ~/.claude.json.
- Workspace source: worktree (cloned from host /Users/jayminwest/Projects/os-eco/burrow).

## Pipeline that works today
1. burrow doctor — green.
2. burrow agents list — claude-code v2.1.133 + sapling v0.3.1 detected; codex flagged absent.
3. burrow fork <project> --task '...' --json — provisions worktree + identity + profile in one shot.
4. burrow prompt <id> '<msg>' --json — spawns claude-code with permissionMode=bypassPermissions, streams NDJSON to stdout while persisting. Run run_tpffbxb49nwv succeeded in 6.1s, 2 turns, $0.106, exit 0.
5. burrow events --follow --burrow <id> --json — live tail confirmed via background monitor.
6. burrow watch --once --json — DashboardSnapshot v1 with full runs[] + eventTail[].
7. burrow logs <id> / burrow show <id> — replay both work; show summarizes runs and recent events.
8. burrow stop <id> — flips state to stopped, workspace persists on disk.

## Sandbox sanity checks
- Agent cwd resolves to <data-dir>/projects/burrow/workspaces/<id>, NOT the host repo — worktree mount confirmed.
- Init event tool list matches the host claude-code; bypassPermissions auto-set by the runtime adapter (commit a2c00ef on the project burrow branch).
- Memory paths root resolves under the workspace, not the host.

## Rough edges (filed as seeds)
- burrow-5d64 — claude-code rate_limit_event and empty 'thinking' events surface as generic text/thinking kinds.
- burrow-469e — system/init payload dominates DashboardSnapshot eventTail[0]; --event-tail-cap doesn't help.

## How to apply
- Minimal end-to-end regression repro: fork -> prompt -> watch/events -> stop. A passing run yields exactly 7 events: state_change, text, thinking, tool_use, tool_result, text, state_change.
- When changing event schema or runtime adapters, re-run this loop and diff the NDJSON against the 7-event shape.
- The DashboardSnapshot init-payload weight is the load-bearing reason watch snapshots feel heavy with many burrows — don't golf event size elsewhere first.

name: runtime-env-passthrough
description: AgentRuntime.envPassthrough?: readonly string[] declares the host env names a built-in runtime's CLI consults at startup; runUpCommand unions those names (skipping agents with forwardCredentials = false) into SandboxProfile.envPassthrough so a project with no burrow.toml [env] block still authenticates. claude-code declares ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN. Per-project keys (DATABASE_URL etc.) still belong in burrow.toml [env].

name: pi-rpc-research-summary
description: pi (@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent v0.74.0) --mode rpc is a JSONL command/event protocol over stdin/stdout, LF-only framed (Node readline non-compliant; burrow's dispatch.ts manual indexOf('\n') is compliant). Commands written one-per-line on stdin: {type:'prompt',message,id?}, steer, follow_up, abort, new_session, get_state, set_model, etc. Events streamed on stdout: agent_start/end, turn_start/end, message_start/update/end (with delta subtypes text_delta/thinking_delta/toolcall_delta), tool_execution_start/update/end, queue_update, compaction_start/end, auto_retry_start/end, extension_error, extension_ui_request (only if extensions enabled), plus {type:'response',command,success,id,error,data} echoes per command. CLI default provider is Google Gemini — must force --provider anthropic for burrow integration. Headless OAuth not supported; API-key env vars per provider (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, ...). --no-session disables session persistence, --no-extensions kills the extension_ui_request dialog protocol (burrow has no stdin-write path to respond mid-stream). Pinned package version v0.74.0, npm bin 'pi' from dist/cli.js. Full research feeds pl-5198 (burrow-8aff).

content: pi v0.74.0 --mode rpc exits the instant stdin closes, even mid-inference. burrow's dispatcher MUST hold stdin open until 'agent_end' arrives on stdout (or send an explicit RPC exit cmd) — diverges from claude-code stream-json which finishes inference after EOF. Reproduce: (echo '{"type":"prompt","message":"..."}'; sleep N) | pi --mode rpc … . Without the hold pi emits only response + agent_start + turn_start + echoed-user message_start/end and exits 0. Contradicts plan pl-5198 risk #9; step burrow-4a3b must wire this explicitly. Fixture under src/runtime/parsers/__golden__/.

pi v0.74.0 auth precedence: stored OAuth at ~/.pi/agent/auth.json (from 'pi /login') wins over ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var. Only explicit '--api-key <value>' argv flag overrides the OAuth path. Plan's envPassthrough-only auth contract (mx-d46d5d) holds inside the burrow sandbox (where ~/.pi is not bind-mounted) but is a footgun in host-mode dev — if a developer ran 'pi /login' on the host their OAuth route silently consumes plan limits regardless of env var. Document in piRuntime install-check or prepareWorkspace.

pi v0.74.0 RPC wire shape (from real captured fixtures under src/runtime/parsers/__golden__/): (1) first event is always {type:'response', command:'prompt', success:true} acking the inbound prompt; (2) assistant content is delta-streamed via message_update envelopes whose assistantMessageEvent.type ∈ {thinking,text,toolcall}_{start,delta,end}, but message_end carries the fully-assembled content[] so the parser can canonicalize on message_end and treat updates as best-effort streaming telemetry; (3) tool results are a dedicated message envelope with message.role==='toolResult' (not a content block inside a user message like claude-code's tool_result blocks); (4) on provider 4xx pi still emits the full message_start→message_end→turn_end→agent_end chain with stopReason:'error' and a string errorMessage carrying the upstream body, with empty assistant content[].

parsePiEvents (src/runtime/parsers/pi.ts) treats pi's message_end with role=assistant as the canonical content carrier — message_update is mapped to telemetry/system as best-effort streaming. The README #3 wire-shape fact (message_end carries the fully assembled content[]) is the contract.

Pi assistant content uses block.type='toolCall' (camelCase, with fields id/name/arguments) where claude-code uses 'tool_use' (snake_case, with fields id/name/input). parsePiEvents normalizes burrow's RunEvent.kind to 'tool_use' but preserves the raw block (camelCase fields intact) in payload — downstream consumers must read .arguments not .input when the kind originated from pi.

Pi parser drops assistant 'thinking' blocks whose 'thinking' string is empty (matches claude-code's burrow-5d64 behavior). Pi emits an empty-text thinking placeholder right before its first tool call; forwarding it would surface noise to consumers. The thinkingSignature field is non-empty even when the text is, so check the text payload, not the signature, when deciding to drop.

Pi event-kind collapse map (src/runtime/parsers/pi.ts): lifecycle (response, agent_start/end, turn_start/end, message_start, tool_execution_start/end, compaction_start/end, extension_error, extension_ui_request) → state_change/system; streaming telemetry (message_update, queue_update, auto_retry_start/end) → telemetry/system; assistant message_end content blocks → text|thinking|tool_use/stdout; toolResult message_end → tool_result/stdout. Unknown envelope types default to state_change/system so additive vocab additions are observable without code changes (SPEC §14.1).

description: piRuntime returns a string stdin (the correct RPC line shape), but the current dispatcher path (src/provider/local/sandbox.ts writeStringStdin) writes the string then immediately closes stdin. pi v0.74.0 exits on stdin close (mx-d9b3ad), so real runs produce only the lifecycle prefix (response + agent_start + turn_start + echoed-user message_start/end) and exit 0 with no assistant content.
resolution: Filed burrow-5db3 to wire stdin-hold in the dispatcher (close stdin only after agent_end on stdout). burrow-56bb (e2e dispatcher test) now blocks on burrow-5db3. The runtime side stays minimal — the wire shape is correct, only the lifecycle plumbing needs change.

content: piRuntime (src/runtime/pi.ts) buildSpawnCommand argv shape is locked: PI_FORCED_ARGV (pi --mode rpc --no-session --no-extensions --provider anthropic) + --model PI_DEFAULT_MODEL (claude-haiku-4-5, matched to golden fixtures). Stdin is a string of newline-joined {"type":"prompt","message":...} RPC envelopes — one for the prompt, one per pending steering message tagged '[STEERING] (priority: P) <body>'. envPassthrough is the anthropic trio only (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/AUTH_TOKEN/BASE_URL); non-anthropic provider keys are deliberately excluded because the provider pin would leak them. supportsResume:false for V1.

BUILT_IN_RUNTIMES (src/runtime/registry.ts) and knownBuiltInIds()/AGENT_ALIASES (src/runtime/aliases.ts) intentionally diverge as of burrow-e950: piRuntime is registered as the 4th built-in but is NOT yet in aliases.ts — `bw init pi` / `bw agents add pi` will still reject `pi` as unknown. Aliases-side wiring (canonical id + any short forms) is scoped to a follow-up under plan pl-5198, not this seed.

Pi RPC handshake compatibility lock lives in src/runtime/parsers/pi-handshake.test.ts and canonicalizes the documented volatile fields (timestamp, responseId, request_id, thinkingSignature, toolCallId — plus the 'id' field inside type:'toolCall' content blocks, which carries the same toolCallId value) before byte-comparing each fixture against a checked-in canonical golden at src/runtime/parsers/__golden__/<fixture>.canonical.jsonl. Sorted-key serialization makes the golden deterministic. To regenerate when pi version bumps: BURROW_UPDATE_PI_GOLDEN=1 bun test src/runtime/parsers/pi-handshake.test.ts. The unit tests in pi.test.ts cover envelope-by-envelope mapping; pi-handshake.test.ts locks the full-trace wire shape.

SPEC §14.1 footnote documents the pi runtime's event-kind collapse: pi's wider RPC vocabulary (agent_*, turn_*, message_start/update/end, tool_execution_*, queue_update, compaction_*, auto_retry_*, extension_*, response acks) collapses losslessly into the SPEC §14.1 stable kinds with the full envelope preserved in payload. Future principled kind-widening (e.g. compaction as first-class kind) is non-breaking because the data is already in payload — matches additive-only posture of mx-cb3d62.

SPEC.md keeps three places in sync when adding a built-in runtime: §12.2 (one-line per-runtime descriptor with spawn shape + supportsResume + steering posture), §14.1 footnote (only if the runtime collapses a wider native event vocabulary into the stable §14.1 kinds), and §22 Phase 4 (the 'Built-ins:' list). Pi was the first runtime to need the §14.1 footnote — claude-code/sapling/codex emit subsets of the stable taxonomy directly.

name: agent-resume-token-propagation
description: Built-in runtimes that support resume implement an optional AgentRuntime.extractMetadata(ctx) hook (src/runtime/runtime.ts) called by the dispatcher post-spawn-success. It returns a partial Record<string, unknown> merged into Run.metadataJson via RunsRepo.patchMetadata. buildResumeCommand later reads the resume token off priorRun.metadataJson. Pi sources its session_id from <workspace>/.pi/sessions/<ts>_<uuid>.jsonl (header line type=session, id=<uuid>); claude-code's analogous source would be the system/init event's session_id field (not yet wired).

content: Pi runtime supportsResume=true (burrow-4d8b lift). PI_FORCED_ARGV swaps --no-session for --session-dir .pi/sessions; prepareWorkspace creates the dir under workspacePath. extractMetadata reads the newest *.jsonl in that dir and pulls 'id' off the first-line {type:'session', id, ...} envelope pi writes synchronously on startup. buildResumeCommand appends --session <id> when priorRun.metadataJson.session_id is set, and keeps the pinned --session-dir so resume reads from the same storage.

AgentRuntime.shouldCloseStdinOnEvent(event) opts a runtime into stdin-hold semantics: the dispatcher writes the prompt + holds stdin, watching each parsed+persisted event for the predicate to match (then calls SpawnResult.closeStdin once, idempotent). pi v0.74.0 sets this to match agent_end envelopes (mx-d9b3ad). Runtimes whose CLI flushes on stdin EOF (claude-code --print) must NOT define it. The dispatcher also closes stdin defensively in its finally block to drop dangling FDs when the trigger never arrives.

AgentRuntime.encodeSteeringMessage(message): { stdin } | undefined is the mid-run steering encoder (SPEC §13.5). The dispatcher's mid-run inbox poll loop calls it per newly-arrived row when the runtime ALSO declares shouldCloseStdinOnEvent (stdin-hold prerequisite) and SpawnResult.writeStdin is available. Returning undefined declines mid-run delivery for that row (stays unread). Pi maps each message to a single '{"type":"prompt","message":"[STEERING] (priority: P) <body>"}\n' line — parity with encodeInboxMessage but per-message and explicitly newline-terminated so pi's NDJSON read loop frames it. Claude-code/sapling/codex leave it unset and keep §13.2/§13.3 next-spawn semantics.

SpawnContext.frontmatter?: AgentFrontmatter ({provider?: string, model?: string}) is the per-run provider/model override channel (burrow-b5b4). The dispatcher (src/runner/dispatch.ts readFrontmatter) hydrates it from Run.metadataJson.frontmatter — that's where upstream callers (e.g. warren's rendered_agent_json.frontmatter) drop the resolved values. Built-in runtimes that accept --provider/--model flags substitute these for their defaults when set; runtimes that don't care just ignore the field. Empty/whitespace strings are treated as unset and fall back to runtime defaults.

piRuntime renders argv via buildPiArgv(frontmatter) (src/runtime/pi.ts): copies PI_FORCED_ARGV, replaces the last 'anthropic' slot with frontmatter.provider when non-empty (trimmed), and appends '--model <frontmatter.model || PI_DEFAULT_MODEL>'. PI_FORCED_ARGV stays bit-for-bit identical to today's frozen prefix (still ending in PI_DEFAULT_PROVIDER='anthropic') — its regression test passes a fresh copy through buildPiArgv and re-asserts the constant didn't mutate. envPassthrough is intentionally NOT widened when a non-anthropic provider override lands; projects opt non-anthropic keys in via burrow.toml [env] (mx-d46d5d).

PI_FORCED_ARGV (src/runtime/pi.ts) pins '--offline' alongside '--no-extensions'. Pi v0.74.0 does startup network operations (telemetry / update checks) BEFORE its stdin RPC reader processes the first prompt; inside bwrap those calls block the read loop for 2+ minutes (burrow-029d). --offline is equivalent to PI_OFFLINE=1; burrow runs are headless and don't surface update banners, so it's pure latency win. Drop only if pi's startup ordering changes.

encodePiStdin and encodeInboxMessage (src/runtime/pi.ts) MUST terminate every JSON envelope with '
', not join them with '
'. Pi's --mode rpc is line-delimited: it buffers stdin and only fires a parsed command on newline. Without a trailing terminator the single-prompt case (every fresh run with no steering messages) sits forever — pi initializes (scaffolds .pi/agent/auth.json, spins libuv workers, parks in epoll_pwait2) but never reaches inference. encodeSteeringMessage already used this convention; encodePiStdin / encodeInboxMessage diverged because their multi-line outputs accidentally worked under 'split + join' until you hit a single-line input. Rule: use lines.map(l => l + '
').join('') (or equivalent) — every line, including the last, ends in 
.

name: runtime-env-passthrough-conditional
description: AgentRuntime.envPassthrough widened from readonly string[] to readonly string[] | ((ctx: EnvPassthroughContext) => readonly string[]) so a runtime that multiplexes over providers (pi --provider <name>) can return a per-run conditional set. burrow up invokes the function with an empty frontmatter to bake the base into SandboxProfile.envPassthrough (respecting forwardCredentials=false); the dispatcher re-invokes it with the run's actual frontmatter (readFrontmatter on Run.metadataJson) via applyRuntimeEnvPassthrough and unions the delta onto a per-spawn profile copy. The function MUST be pure — no host env reads, no side effects — so the up-time and dispatch-time callers agree on the base set. piEnvPassthrough is the reference impl: anthropic triple as base, plus PI_PROVIDER_ENV_KEYS[provider] (openai→OPENAI_API_KEY, gemini→GEMINI_API_KEY, google→GOOGLE_API_KEY, groq→GROQ_API_KEY, mistral→MISTRAL_API_KEY, deepseek→DEEPSEEK_API_KEY) on top, matched case-insensitively. Static-array runtimes (claude-code, sapling, codex) keep their existing semantics — the type widening is additive.
